source.title: Social play behavior shapes the development of prefrontal inhibition in a region-specific manner

confidence_rationale: Direct whole-cell evidence that play deprivation during a bounded juvenile window (P21-P42) selectively reduces prefrontal inhibition, with region-specific timing (mPFC change manifests during the window, OFC change only after). Strong for the 'bounded window + sub-circuit-specific trajectory' part of the candidate cluster. Discounted because it is prefrontal E/I, not striatal commitment-loop gains, and rodent-only.
failure_signatures: The OFC effect of play deprivation only becomes manifest AFTER the deprivation window closes -- a model assuming all play-calibrated structure is readable at window-close, with no delayed-consolidation dynamics, mispredicts this.
Excitatory transmission was unaffected; only inhibitory transmission was reduced -- a calibration model that tunes gain symmetrically across E and I would be unsupported.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Social play deprivation between P21 and P42 reduces inhibitory (not excitatory) synaptic transmission in both mPFC and OFC, but with region-specific timing: the mPFC reduction occurs during deprivation whereas the OFC reduction only appears after the deprivation period; prefrontal subregions follow different developmental trajectories.
mapping.ree_translation: Play acts within a bounded juvenile window to set inhibitory gain, and different prefrontal subregions (which feed different cortico-striatal loops) have different windows and different consolidation latencies. This maps onto REE's prediction that the three commitment loops (ARC-021/MECH-069) are not calibrated synchronously -- each has its own window -- and to ARC-023's claim that the loops run on distinct timescales.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Measured at prefrontal pyramidal cells, not at striatal loop nodes; 'loop-specific window' is inferred from region-specific prefrontal trajectories, not measured at the striatum. The delayed-OFC effect is suggestive of a consolidation/lock latency but the paper does not characterise a lock mechanism.
mapping.source_context: Whole-cell patch-clamp recordings in layer-5 pyramidal neurons of juvenile (P21), adolescent (P42) and adult (P85) rats, mPFC and OFC, after P21-P42 social play deprivation.

confidence_components.notes: Good electrophysiological rigour. Mapping fidelity moderate: the region-specific-window finding is the cleanest available analogue of differential loop windows, but it is prefrontal not striatal. Transfer risk moderate-high (rodent; loop-level inference).
